steps.title: Calculate vitamin B12 per ounce for each food
steps.content: Yogurt provides 1 microgram per 4.0 ounces, so the rate is $\frac{1}{4.0}$ micrograms per ounce. Salmon provides 1 microgram per 1.5 ounces, so the rate is $\frac{1}{1.5}$ micrograms per ounce.

steps.title: Set up the equation based on total B12 consumed
steps.content: If x ounces of yogurt are consumed and y ounces of salmon are consumed, the total B12 is: $$\frac{1}{4.0}x + \frac{1}{1.5}y = 2.1$$

steps.title: Convert to decimal form to match the choices
steps.content: $\frac{1}{4.0} = 0.25$ and $\frac{1}{1.5} \approx 0.67$, so the equation becomes $$0.25x + 0.67y = 2.1$$ That matches Choice B — $0.25x + 0.67y = 2.1$.
